situational_context: Early rebuild phase, Week 3 post-return-to-running. Chronic load up 25% since bootstrap (17.8→22.3), driven by e-bike commuting and occasional structured sessions (1 gravel ride, 2 return-to-running sessions). Run #1 (May 3, 2.47 km) and Run #2 (May 9, 2.01 km) completed without reported pain. Weight trending down steadily (106.9→104.1 kg over 23d, −2.6%). ACWR 0.75 below optimal band — acute load normalizing after May 1 gravel spike (load 55) rolled out of the window. Training status shows 'Detraining' but this reflects low dedicated-session volume on commute-heavy weeks, not fitness loss. Readiness consistently high (median 82). Life-constrained by young baby (Léonie, 16 months) and 'sur site' e-bike commute days. VO2max running adjusted 40.9→38.3. Protein intake persistently below 150g target (avg 115g/d).

recommendations.text: Increase daily protein to ≥130g — current avg 115g/d leaves a 23% gap vs 150g target. At 104 kg with active running comeback, protein supports tendon collagen synthesis and lean mass preservation during caloric deficit.
Execute Run #3 Friday if zero pain since Run #2 (May 9). Self-assess Achilles, patellar tendon, tibia, plantar fascia before starting. If any doubt, substitute with 45 min Z2 vélo.
recommendations.measurable: Run #3 completed at ≤2.5 km, avg HR ≤145 bpm, or substituted with vélo if pain

recommendations.text: Prioritize both weekend cycling sessions (Sat+Sun) — primary load-builders this week. Target combined load ≥28 to help ACWR recover toward 0.80 band.
recommendations.measurable: Both weekend sessions executed with combined load ≥28

markers_to_watch.reason: Currently 0.75, below optimal band. Four consecutive 'Detraining' readings. Weekend sessions should help correct.
Down 2.8 kg in 23d (≈0.85 kg/wk) — good pace. Faster loss would compromise tendon adaptation during running comeback.
Return-to-running Week 3 at 104 kg. Each run is a tissue-tolerance test. Any pain >24h → skip next run, stay on vélo.
